text: I'm Adam Ramsey PT.
I am competing for Team England at this year's Commonwealth Games.
I am triple Olympic champion, double world record holder, but I'm still wanting more.
PT in four has had a fantastic season and looks super in the semis.
Wow, I can still remember this race.
To the final, the men's 100m breaststroke.
PT has to hold his stroke.
This is where I start to go now.
And Adam Peaty for England is coming through.
Can Adam Peaty win the gold?
I think he's going to.
Yes, he does.
He beats the world record holder.
He beats the Olympic champion.
And he's just gone sub -59 seconds to win the Commonwealth Games 100m breaststroke.
Still a boy there, essentially 19 years old.
I know that race and I can remember it because it was my first big win, first senior championships, first gold at that level.
Imagine if I made a mistake, you know, would my career look the same?
But yeah, what an incredible thing to reflect on, especially going into my fourth Commonwealth Games now.
2026 has already been one of those years our wedding was, you know, one of the best days of my life.
you know, watching Holly walk down the aisle.
It's a view that I'll never ever forget.
It's almost like I've got a snapshot in my brain and the music in the church was just incredible.
But yeah, having Gordon as my father -in -law is an incredible blessing.
So my life has changed quite a lot.
I'm also expecting a baby little girl.
Well, I'm incredibly excited.
For me, that role of being a father has been just incredible and to have another human in this world
that loves and cherishes you and I think it's the biggest blessing.
My love for them isn't going to be judged by a medal and obviously with Holly going through pregnancy
all I can pray for is her happiness and the health as well as a baby and for me that does push me even more to I guess grow up.
You haven't forgotten how to change a nappy?
Oh no.
no.
Representing England, Adam Breity!
2014 was my first Commonwealth Games, my first year of breaking world records.
A lot has changed in the last 12 years.
Obviously when you break world records and you get Olympic golds, you kind of get more public eye on you.
And for me, that can be intrusive, but I just see it as I'm a person that is good at my job with a by -product.
Not many people know this but around March time 2023
I did three lengths of breaststroke as part of my warm -up and on the last length my goggles filled up with tears.
I lost the love for for everything that I live for.
Sport is why I live and breathe and something I do every single day.
And it's helped me enormously in my life.
But when that same sport breaks you and breaks you down and it's something you start to hate, it's an unimaginable pain that you have to rebuild yourself.
But what I have put in place there is balance because how can I do it for another two or another four or an 06,
whatever that timeframe is going to be, is that, for me, balance is going to be incredibly important.

today.
If you are going to work with Adam you have to be prepared to walk into Adam's world
where he is extremely intense and focused on what he wants to achieve
and as a result he never switches off from the sport and his expectation on you as a coach is exactly the same.
Legs still a little low on entry.
It's expected isn't it?
Yeah okay but definitely in terms of like projection.
I think it's definitely Adam's mindset.
He has an ability to go into predator mode.
It's like perfect, down for a few days, up, into Rome.
He is able to read a race, know exactly what he needs to do, where his mindset needs to go.
His body is merely just the vessel when it comes to his race and actually it's his mind that is steering himself.
I think the thing that has set me apart most of my career and something that is still setting me apart is my emotion.
Knowing that I've got a finite amount of time in the sport left and really putting that pressure on myself.
Because yes, this sport will break you, guaranteed, if you do it for as long as I have.
And I have been broken.
Through working with him this year, he is a completely different person from the Adam in 2014.

he's not only older and wiser but he is an ultimate competitor and he is extremely focused and everything that he does and says that he wants to do he speaks with such conviction and determination to where he wants to go
